presetName: Archetype distillate — wide evolving cinematic pad (logic-only, not a vendor preset)
style: cinematic wide evolving pad with controlled motion

mappings.designIntent: Prioritize sustained harmonic beds that evolve over bars, not hits: the ear should feel time passing inside the sound. Width is a feature when it stays musically correlated; mono compatibility still matters for club and broadcast.
mappings.humanPerception: Listeners map slow filter motion to 'breathing' or 'opening curtains'; chorus-ish width reads as 'huge' only when the midrange stays defined. Mud is the failure mode when lows stack without movement—this archetype keeps lows either rolled or moving slowly so the brain separates evolution from rumble.
mappings.contrastWithLesson001: Where aggressive bass (v1) optimizes attack and short decay, this archetype inverts the priority: sustain and release carry the story, attack is often soft or blurred, and brightness is modulated over seconds, not milliseconds.
mappings.ampEnvelope: Attack softened (fade-in, slow attack, or noise-ramped) so the pad does not click or slap unless intentionally cinematic; decay long; sustain high so the harmonic cloud persists; release long and often the main expressive tail—tail shapes whether the sound feels 'huge' or 'waspy'.
mappings.oscillatorArchitecture: Stack detuned saws or hybrids with a noise or granular layer for air; optional fifth or octave reinforcement for 'church' width; wavetable position may drift slowly—motion at the source before filtering.
mappings.harmonicDensity: Rich partials are acceptable because the filter and motion carve space; harshness is managed by movement (filter rides resonance down) rather than by stripping harmonics statically.
mappings.filterTrajectory: Cutoff sweeps over multi-bar phrases; resonance used for vowel peaks or 'cry' moments—not narrow resonant screaming unless genre calls for it; key-tracking decisions affect whether chords stay balanced or one note dominates.
mappings.filterModulationSources: LFO to cutoff at sub-audio to low-musical rates; envelope follower optional for sidechain-like ducking without kick audio; step sequencers rare—prefer smooth or drunken curves for cinematic feel.
mappings.lfoRatesAndShapes: Multiple LFOs at non-integer ratios to avoid obvious repetition; triangle/sine for predictable breathe, sample-hold or smoothed random for organic drift; sync-to-tempo for 'scene changes' on bar boundaries.
mappings.envelopeModMatrix: Secondary envelopes shape filter depth, wavetable index, or FM amount so evolution has chapters—intro bloom vs held plateau vs outro darken.
mappings.fxReverbAndSpace: Long predelay separates source from wash; size and decay scaled so the dry-ish core remains locatable; high-frequency damping prevents ice-pick tails; mono-low or EQ-safe low cut in reverb return to protect mix foundation.
mappings.fxDelay: Dotted or triplet delays for rhythmic halo without turning the pad into a lead; filtered feedback so repeats darken—supports evolution without new notes.
mappings.fxChorusUnisonStereo: Chorus/unison widen detuned copies; when overdone, phase collapse in mono—keep a mono-sum check; Haas-only width is a last resort and needs high-frequency band limiting.
mappings.distortionSaturationInPads: Soft saturation thickens mid harmonics for speaker-friendly 'glow'; hard clipping avoided unless stylized; drive often post-filter so highs are controlled before saturation.
mappings.voiceAllocationAndUnison: Voice count trades CPU for thickness; detune spread vs perceived 'supersaw blur'; spread that is too wide collapses center—bias some energy to mono mids for vocal compatibility.
mappings.pitchDriftAndHumanization: Subtle per-voice drift or micro-detune prevents static laser-beam sterility; too much reads as out-of-tune—tune to context (analog woozy vs digital pristine).
mappings.sidechainGhost: Invisible pump via external ducking shapes pads under kicks without obvious EDM pumping—depth and release tuned to genre.
mappings.motionSemantics: Motion should imply narrative: 'opening', 'closing', 'tension', 'release'—mapped to filter+detune+reverb bloom, not random wobble without phrase alignment.
mappings.spectralBalance: High air vs low weight: pads eat mix space—high-pass or dynamic EQ on the source or bus to stop sub build-up; presence bump optional for lyric intelligibility overlap.
mappings.monoCompatibilityCheck: Regular mono fold-down audit: chorused pads often lose center—ensure lead-ish lines or chord roots remain if the genre requires mono clubs.
mappings.dynamicExpression: Velocity or CC maps to filter or layer blend so live play feels musical; static max velocity sounds like a demo loop.
mappings.noiseAndGrainLayer: Filtered noise or granular shimmer for 'air' that moves independently of oscillator pitch—helps large stages without adding more saw detune.
mappings.formantAndVocalImplication: If resonance or comb filtering creates vowel-like peaks, lean into it for 'choir pad' semantics; otherwise de-emphasize to avoid talking-pad distraction under vocals.
mappings.rhythmicChoppingVsContinuous: Gated pads are a different archetype—this lesson assumes continuous sustain with optional tremolo, not 16th-note chop unless explicitly layered as a secondary texture.
mappings.mixRole: Background bed that can swell to near-foreground on builds; must not mask kick/snare transients—EQ and sidechain discipline; often high-passed relationship with bass (v1) occupying sub.
mappings.failureModes: Common bad outcomes: static supersaw + big reverb = cheap; uncontrolled sub = mud; too-fast LFO = seasick; phasey width = mono collapse; infinite shimmer without harmonic story = wallpaper.
mappings.translationToPromptTokens: Human words like 'lush', 'evolving', 'cinematic', 'wide', 'breathing' map to slow modulation + long envelopes + stereo depth + controlled lows—teach models to bind adjectives to these control families.
mappings.aiHumanBridgeSummary: Models infer macro intent ('make it bigger')—humans translate to specific families: attack softening, filter sweep depth, reverb predelay, stereo spread law, low-end hygiene. This mapping block is the reusable teaching object.

character: A tall, slowly breathing harmonic cloud: soft attack into a sustained, wide bed with clear midrange identity and airy top; motion feels like curtains opening and closing rather than twitchy EDM wobble; lows are present as weight but rarely as uncontrolled sub mud; stereo depth reads as cinematic scale while a mono-safe core keeps chords and emotional center intelligible in headphones and clubs alike.
causalReasoning:
Evolving pads read as 'cinematic' because the ear tracks slow change over time: long sustain and release let partials beat and filter peaks move in ways our auditory scene analysis interprets as environment and emotion rather than rhythm. Soft attack prevents transient competition with drums and vocals, so the pad occupies a different time slot than lesson-001 bass archetypes.

Width from chorus/unison/detune increases interaural difference at mid frequencies, which the brain maps to 'space' and 'ensemble size'—but uncorrelated widening collapses in mono, so controlled spread plus mid mono anchoring preserves translation. Filter motion ties brightness to phrase structure: when cutoff rises, harmonics increase and the sound 'opens'; when it falls, the texture 'closes' or 'withdraws'—semantic labels follow spectral centroid movement.

Reverb and delay extend the temporal horizon: predelay separates direct timbre from wash so the source remains localizable; long decay extends emotional tail without necessarily increasing loudness. Saturation adds harmonic fill that reads as 'warmth' on small speakers where fundamentals are weak.

Modulation at musically slow rates avoids the vestibular 'seasick' cue of fast pitch/filter modulation. Multiple LFOs at irrational ratios create non-repeating evolution, which feels organic compared to a single synced triangle that loops obviously every bar.

Together, these families implement a coherent inverse of transient-first bass design: time-domain emphasis shifts from attack to sustain and release; spectral emphasis shifts from bite to evolving centroid; spatial emphasis shifts from centered punch to correlated width—with shared discipline around low-end hygiene so neither archetype destroys the mix foundation.
coreRules: Soft attack + high sustain + long release → the story is evolution in time, not punch on the one.
Slow filter motion tied to phrasing → brightness and emotion move in bars, not in transient rhythm.
Predelayed wash + low-end discipline → size without mud; bad mapping is fast LFO + uncontrolled sub (failureModes in mappings).

contrastWith.difference: Lesson 2 optimizes sustained beds, spectral drift, and space; lesson 1 optimizes attack, compact body, and mix-forward bass. The decision fork: need impact and slot under drums (lean lesson 1 time axis) vs need evolving harmonic scenery (lean lesson 2 time axis)—same hygiene, different irreducible core.
